skill.name: scanpy
skill.description: Guides single-cell RNA-seq analysis with Scanpy, including data loading, QC, preprocessing, dimensionality reduction, clustering, marker discovery, annotation, and plotting.
skill.summary: Run structured Scanpy workflows for single-cell RNA-seq analysis and visualization.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown code fences and Scanpy scientific terms, not executable shell backticks or weak cryptography. The only confirmed risk is legitimate local filesystem use for reading datasets, creating figure folders, and writing analysis outputs.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Writes for Analysis Outputs
low_findings.description: The bundled Python scripts create output directories and write h5ad, CSV, and figure files. This is expected for a Scanpy analysis workflow, but users should choose output paths carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit and local. They support normal analysis output rather than persistence, exfiltration, or system modification.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command-execution patterns occur in Markdown code fences and inline Scanpy API examples. No evidence found that the skill executes shell backticks or constructs commands from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed examples are fenced Python or Bash snippets for user workflows. They are documentation text, not executable skill logic.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are Scientific-Term False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity cryptography detections align with Scanpy and scRNA-seq terminology or ordinary code locations. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited contexts are descriptions, Scanpy names, argparse setup, or AnnData accessors. None implement cryptographic primitives.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are static links to official Scanpy, tutorial, and scverse documentation. No evidence found of HTTP requests, data upload, telemetry, or credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a resource list. Search found no runtime network libraries or request calls in the skill files.

content.value_statement: Single-cell RNA-seq analysis requires many connected preprocessing, clustering, and visualization steps. This skill provides Scanpy workflows, references, and scripts that guide reproducible analysis from raw data to annotated results.
actual_capabilities: Loads h5ad, 10X HDF5, 10X matrix, and CSV single-cell datasets through Scanpy examples.
Guides quality control with mitochondrial metrics, cell filtering, gene filtering, and QC plots.
Documents normalization, log transformation, highly variable gene selection, scaling, and regression steps.
Shows PCA, UMAP, t-SNE, neighborhood graph, Leiden clustering, and marker gene workflows.
Provides plotting guidance for QC, embeddings, marker genes, trajectories, and publication-ready figures.
Includes a QC script and a full analysis template that users can adapt locally.
limitations: It depends on local Python packages such as scanpy, pandas, numpy, and matplotlib.
It does not automatically validate biological cell type labels against external databases.
Large datasets may require memory tuning beyond the included examples.
The bundled scripts read and write local files, so paths should be reviewed before running.

use_cases.title: Initial Dataset Quality Control
use_cases.description: Run a consistent QC workflow to inspect mitochondrial percentage, gene counts, total counts, and filtering thresholds.

use_cases.title: Cluster Discovery and Marker Review
use_cases.description: Build a Scanpy pipeline for normalization, dimensionality reduction, Leiden clustering, and ranked marker gene analysis.

use_cases.title: Publication Figure Preparation
use_cases.description: Create UMAP, heatmap, dot plot, violin, trajectory, and multi-panel figures with documented Scanpy plotting settings.

prompt_templates.title: Start a QC Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Scanpy skill to help me inspect an h5ad dataset, choose QC thresholds, and generate before-and-after QC plots.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Standard Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Scanpy workflow for my dataset that covers loading, QC, normalization, HVG selection, PCA, UMAP, Leiden clustering, and marker genes.

prompt_templates.title: Annotate Cell Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Scanpy references to help annotate Leiden clusters from marker genes and produce plots that support each assignment.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Advanced Analysis Choices
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Scanpy analysis plan and suggest parameter changes for batch effects, clustering resolution, trajectory analysis, and publication figures.

output_examples.input: I need to run QC on a new 10X dataset before clustering.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step QC plan with loading guidance, mitochondrial metrics, threshold choices, filtering checks, and saved QC plot recommendations.

output_examples.input: My Leiden clusters are ready, but I need marker gene plots.
output_examples.output: A marker review workflow with ranked genes, UMAP overlays, dot plots, heatmaps, and notes for validating cluster annotations.

output_examples.input: I need publication-ready figures from my Scanpy object.
output_examples.output: A plotting checklist covering figure resolution, vector formats, color palettes, legends, multi-panel layouts, and export locations.

best_practices: Save raw counts before normalization so later expression plots can use the appropriate data layer.
Inspect QC distributions before setting thresholds, because good cutoffs vary by tissue, platform, and sample quality.
Validate cell type annotations with multiple marker genes and document the evidence for each cluster.
anti_patterns: Do not reuse default QC thresholds without checking dataset-specific distributions.
Do not interpret clusters as cell types from one marker gene alone.
Do not overwrite important h5ad files before confirming output paths and filenames.

faq.question: What data formats does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers h5ad, 10X HDF5, 10X matrix directories, CSV, and common AnnData workflows documented in Scanpy.

faq.question: Does this skill run Scanpy automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance, references, a QC script, and an analysis template. Execution depends on your local Python environment.

faq.question: Can it help choose QC thresholds?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains common thresholds and emphasizes reviewing dataset-specific QC plots before filtering cells and genes.

faq.question: Can it annotate cell types automatically?
faq.answer: It supports manual annotation with marker genes, but biological validation remains the user responsibility.

faq.question: Does it support trajectory analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. The references include PAGA and diffusion pseudotime examples for exploratory trajectory workflows.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The confirmed behavior is local dataset reading and output writing. Static command, crypto, and network alerts were reviewed as false positives.
